commands.repair_reason: Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are compatibility, security-boundary, dependency-supply-chain, and contributor-proof decisions rather than one narrow safe repair loop.; Needs attention: The diff needs security attention because it removes OpenClaw's backend selection/fail-closed policy while moving user-supplied image processing behind Rastermill's auto external backend chain.; Review finding:... (sha=a5940c516520edd8eb4d132a449a32dd07707362)
commands.review_summary:
The branch moves OpenClaw image probing, resizing, encoding, and managed-image limit handling onto rastermill@0.3.0, removes local Photon/parser fallback code, updates dependency locks and shrinkwraps, and tweaks CI/script follow-ups.

PR surface: Source -1109, Tests -662, Docs 0, Config +9, Other -182. Total -1944 across 26 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Source-reproducible for the review findings: current main catches unavailable image processors and exposes backend selection, while the PR head removes those paths; I did not run a live backend-unavailable image flow in the read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are compatibility, security-boundary, dependency-supply-chain, and contributor-proof decisions rather than one narrow safe repair loop.

target.title: Refactor image processing onto Rastermill
target.body:
## Summary

- Delegate OpenClaw image resize/encode/probe mechanics to Rastermill and remove local image parser/codec fallback code.
- Update OpenClaw to the published `rastermill@0.3.0` npm release, including the unified `encode(input, options?)` API for exact formats, byte budgets, auto format selection, dimension limits, metadata policy, and small-limit byte-budget downscaling.
- Keep OpenClaw policy in OpenClaw: media caps, managed attachment warnings, SDK compatibility names, and orientation-aware managed attachment limits.
- Align AWS transitive shrinkwrap resolution with pnpm's release-age-held lock versions so npm shrinkwrap generation remains reproducible after the root dependency change.
- Fix PR CI follow-ups already on this branch: satisfy the Opengrep `git add --` guard and run the Windows node lane on Node 22.19.0, matching the supported floor and current Blacksmith Windows image.

## Verification

- Rastermill: `pnpm check`
- Rastermill: `pnpm test` (2 files / 57 tests)
- Rastermill: `pnpm build`
- Rastermill: `pnpm docs:check`
- Rastermill: `npm pack --dry-run`
- Rastermill GitHub CI: run `26471848289` passed for `19be07c04392cfd26397ef9acdd6322cfe127a09`.
- Rastermill npm verify: `npm view rastermill@0.3.0 version dist-tags.latest dist.tarball dist.integrity time --json`
- Rastermill release: https://github.com/openclaw/rastermill/releases/tag/v0.3.0
- OpenClaw: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/media/image-ops.rastermill-adapter.test.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts src/agents/tool-images.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/screenshot.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --no-file-parallelism` (7 files / 152 tests)
- OpenClaw: `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -b tsconfig.projects.json`
- OpenClaw: `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed`
- OpenClaw: `git diff --check`
- OpenClaw: `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Image processing mechanics now live in Rastermill instead of OpenClaw-local parser/codec/fallback stacks.
Real environment tested: local macOS checkout, Node/OpenClaw repo wrappers, published `rastermill@0.3.0`, Rastermill release `v0.3.0`, and OpenClaw branch head `a5940c5165`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest media/browser/agent/managed-image tests, full project typecheck, changed gate, Rastermill test/check/build/docs/pack, npm publish verification, and Codex autoreview.
Evidence after fix: Rastermill passed 57 tests and GitHub CI; npm registry reports `rastermill@0.3.0` as latest with integrity `sha512-4g2i0I7M5sba//lFBh19Wi0hDGw8o+isnt/BtEyqQXIZaYclhcNBwL/Fw/6gDCp7aaLwQHADuUvyHCB0Oat5Vw==`; OpenClaw focused media/browser/agent/managed-image set passed 7 files / 152 tests; `check:changed` passed including shrinkwrap guard, typecheck, lint, and import cycles; autoreview ended clean.
Observed result after fix: OpenClaw call sites now use Rastermill's unified `encode` API for PNG byte-budget search, web media auto optimization, and managed image dimension limits while keeping OpenClaw-specific policy at the call sites.
What was not tested: no live HEIC conversion fixture against installed native tools in this PR run.
target.author: steipete
